blockers.title: Published events can omit the required place or join details
blockers.evidence: Spec A1-A3 requires an event to carry `venue/city or meeting_link` and ship the member event detail experience with no coming-soon surfaces (`specs/panel-experience.spec.md:27-51`). The vault workshop design says the MVP host brings the meeting tool and pasted link (`02 Workshop System - Roles, Setup & Lifecycle (Design).md:13,180,210`). Code does not enforce that: `upsertEvent` accepts optional `meeting_link`, `venue`, and `city` (`convex/admin/events.ts:196-209`), validates only a non-empty meeting link (`convex/admin/events.ts:258-266`), and `publishEvent` flips draft to published without revalidating logistics (`convex/admin/events.ts:395-429`). The admin form also only rejects a non-empty non-https link, then sends undefined for empty online links or in-person venue/city (`src/admin/views/EventEditor.tsx:109-115,247-250`). The test suite currently blesses this gap by publishing an online event created from `upsertArgs()` with no `meeting_link` (`tests/convex/events.test.ts:124-132,567-580`).
blockers.required_fix: Add server-side publish/upsert validation that online published events require a bounded https `meeting_link`, and in-person published events require the member-facing venue/city fields required by the spec. Mirror it in the admin form only for UX, and add tests proving publish refuses missing logistics for both formats.

non_blocking_notes: `npm run typecheck -- --pretty false` passed in this read-only review.
`git diff --check main...HEAD` passed, apart from sandbox xcrun cache warnings.
Secret/em-dash scan found no committed secrets and no diff-added member-facing em dashes; the only dash hit was a README table placeholder.
Full Gate 5 proof (`npm run check`, `npm test`, `npm run test:e2e`, `npm run build`) was not run here because the sandbox is read-only and those commands may write build/test caches.

spec_compliance.criterion: A1-A3 Events deliver full schema, member event detail, publish flow, and no coming-soon surfaces
spec_compliance.evidence: Live publication can succeed with no online join link and no in-person location, so the code does not actually deliver the promised event logistics.

spec_compliance.criterion: Under-18 safeguards for events, opportunities, and directory
spec_compliance.evidence: Event lane gating blocks restricted_unknown and separates minor/youth from adult lanes in `convex/events.ts:37-52`; opportunity board/apply gates active standard/ally only in `convex/opportunities.ts:88-103,218-239`; directory blocks minors/restricted_unknown as viewers and listed rows in `convex/directory.ts:7-15,57-87`.

spec_compliance.criterion: Admin authorization, super-admin boundaries, and contact minimization
spec_compliance.evidence: Admin checks resolve the signed-in member server-side, deny by default, and separate admin from super_admin in `convex/lib/adminAuth.ts:43-113`; certificate admin is super-admin-only in `convex/admin/certificates.ts:42-55,87-103`; member list/dossier keep contact masked and route reveal through audited one-member access in `convex/admin/members.ts:9-16,101-150`.

spec_compliance.criterion: Opportunity applications, one-application rule, late refusal, and notifications
spec_compliance.evidence: `convex/opportunities.ts:214-327` trims and bounds statements, enforces lane/audience/state/deadline/profile gates, reuses the unique member-opportunity row, audits, logs activity, notifies receipt, and triggers standing promotion.

spec_compliance.criterion: Brand locks and secrets in the diff
spec_compliance.evidence: No committed secret patterns were found in the reviewed diff, and no diff-added member-facing em dashes were found.

summary: Authorization, safeguarding, data minimization, and audit boundaries look materially sound, but the slice fails the event acceptance criteria because admins can publish unusable live events without the required join/place details.
